title: AI-Driven Resume Quality & ATS Analysis System
description: Built a multi-agent AI pipeline using LangGraph, LangChain, and Docling to ingest resumes, LinkedIn exports, GitHub metadata, and transcripts into a structured PostgreSQL knowledge graph, then scored candidate-to-job alignment using an ATS engine powered by sentence-transformers and scikit-learn. Engineered prompt workflows across OpenAI and Ollama models to generate explainable gap analyses and resume improvements grounded strictly in existing candidate evidence, with validation logic to prevent hallucinated credentials or fabricated experience. Delivered the system through a Textual terminal UI supporting interactive candidate review, with reproducible ETL pipelines and documented prompt engineering practices for reliable AI-assisted hiring analysis.

title: California Real Estate Price Prediction
description: Built a machine learning web application that predicts single-family residence prices across California using ensemble models including XGBoost and LightGBM, trained on historical real estate data with features such as location, square footage, and property characteristics. Developed an interactive Streamlit dashboard with dynamic search and Plotly visualizations, enabling users to input property details and receive price predictions in real time. Evaluated multiple regression approaches with scikit-learn to select the best-performing model, optimizing for predictive accuracy across California's diverse housing markets.

title: eCommerce Session Review Analysis - Predicting Online Shopper Purchase Intent
description: Built classification models using Random Forest and XGBoost on 12,330 e-commerce browsing sessions to predict purchase intent, applying threshold tuning to optimize recall and reduce missed conversions, achieving ~90% accuracy. Applied K-Means clustering to segment shoppers into five behavioral archetypes, uncovering conversion rates ranging from 0.4% for bounce-prone visitors to 24.3% for high-intent consumers. Identified product browsing depth and administrative page engagement as the strongest purchase predictors using Python, scikit-learn, pandas, and seaborn.

title: Dsc207miniproject
description: This project applied data wrangling, statistical analysis, and visualization techniques to a structured dataset using Python libraries including pandas, NumPy, matplotlib, and seaborn. Data was retrieved via the requests library and processed to extract meaningful patterns, with findings communicated through detailed plots and charts. The project demonstrated proficiency in the full data science workflow, from ingestion and cleaning through exploratory analysis and visual storytelling.

title: Dsc215
description: Due to limited information available about this project's specific content, here is a general description based on the course context: Completed coursework and assignments for DSC215 using Jupyter Notebooks, applying data science techniques such as statistical analysis, data wrangling, and visualization to structured datasets. Implemented Python-based workflows to extract insights and draw conclusions from data. The project demonstrated proficiency in core data science methodologies covered throughout the course.

title: Sleepercelmanual
description: Built a manual, notebook-based implementation of a sleeper cell detection system using Python, NumPy, and Pandas to process and evaluate network or behavioral data via the Requests library for data retrieval. The project applied hand-crafted logic and step-by-step data transformations to identify dormant or low-activity entities that periodically activate, mirroring concepts from threat detection and anomaly identification. This manual approach served as a foundational reference implementation, making the underlying methodology transparent and interpretable before potential automation or scaling.

title: SleeperCel
description: Built a full-stack AI-assisted fantasy football roster management tool that integrates with the public Sleeper API to display a user's leagues, rosters, and available players in real time. The frontend was developed using React and Vite, while the backend leveraged Python with Pandas and NumPy to process player data and support roster-building recommendations. The project combined live API data retrieval with data analysis pipelines to help users make informed lineup decisions.

title: Bike Traffic in Cambridge Interactive Visualization
description: Built an interactive visualization of bicycle traffic patterns across Cambridge using JavaScript, CSS, and HTML. Implemented dynamic filtering and map-based rendering to display bike lane usage and traffic volume across different times and locations throughout the city. The project enabled users to identify high-traffic cycling corridors and underutilized infrastructure through intuitive visual controls.

title: Desktop Tutorial
description: This introductory project demonstrated the core Git and GitHub workflow using GitHub Desktop, including initializing a repository, editing a README file, and committing changes to version control. The project followed a guided tutorial structure to practice fundamental source control concepts such as staging, committing, and syncing local changes to a remote repository. It served as a foundational exercise in establishing a reproducible development environment and documentation habits using Markdown.

title: How Airbnb Tourism was impacted by the San Diego Sunshine
description: A static graph which explores Airbnb bookings and revenue throughout the year in San Diego, California. The graph shows how the number of bookings and revenue changes throughout the year, with the highest peaks in the summer months.

title: Study on the Impact of Estrus on Female Rats Throughout the Day
description: This graph is a static visualization of the body temperature across male and female mice. Used to explore differences in the daily cylce of body temperature while highlighting the impact of female estrus.

title: AllRecipes Rating Predictive Classification Model
description: This machine learning model predicts the ratings of recipes on AllRecipes.com based on features of the recipe. We created a multiclass-classifier using a RandomForest.

title: Study of the Presence of Educational Institutions and Income Throughout California
description: An exploratory data analysis of the presence of educational institutions and income throughout California. The project uses a heatmap to show the distribution of educational institutions and income throughout the state of California.

title: Image Processor
description: Built an image processor that can read in an image, apply a filter to it, and then output the processed image. The image processor can apply filters such as negate, grayscale, and edge detection. This project was built using Python.

title: Asteroid Landfall Simulation
description: Written using pandas in the Jupyter Notebook environment, this project simulates the landfall of asteroids using historical datasets, probabilistic modeling, and bootstrapping.

title: Interactive Visualization of Mice Body Temperature Across Gender
description: An interactive visualization of the body temperature across the gender of mice. The visualization allows users to explore the differences in body temperature while allowing filtering and the altering of scope. Features a tooltip which highlights Mouse ID allowing us to examine indvidual mice along with their estrus cycle( should they be female).
